what_it_does: Scans a fixed ledger of chrome surface files for policed geometry declarations, compares px literals against --dna spacing, radius, and type addresses extracted from surface/styles/vars.css, fails on standalone raw literals that exactly match an available address, and reports off-scale values as a non-failing vocabulary ledger.
observations: Reads the active scale from generated CSS variables instead of hardcoding spacing, radius, and type maps.
Polices padding, margin, gap, row-gap, column-gap, border-radius variants, and font-size.
Skips configured generated marker blocks and exempts env() values plus selected px constants for hairlines, pills, and touch floors.
Treats declarations already containing var(--dna-) as addressed for any px tokens in that declaration.
Counts exact-address values inside shorthands as off-scale ledger entries with a *shorthand suffix instead of fixable violations.
Provides --all for full fixable output and --offscale for standalone off-scale spacing rollout output.
Exits with status 1 only when fixable exact-address raw literals are found.
standouts: The file acts as both an enforcing lint and a reporting ledger.
Scale addresses are derived from vars.css at runtime.
Off-scale spacing, type, and radius values are reported without failing the build.
conventions: Uses ESM imports from Node builtins.
Uses uppercase constants for configuration and mutable counters for scan totals.
Uses regex-based CSS declaration scanning rather than a CSS parser.
Reports through console output and process exit codes.
Keeps all scanning behavior file-local in a single script.
concerns: The declaration scanner is not parser-aware, so it can miss complex syntax or match declaration-like text inside JavaScript strings.
Property classification regexes are not anchored, so substring matches can classify unintended property names if they enter the declaration matcher.
Line numbers are based on the declaration start, not the exact px token position.
Numeric scale keys are integer px values, so decimal type values are not addressable through the current map shape.
The surface count in the summary is the configured list length even when missing files are skipped.
notes: This is a geometry-address lint that enforces exact --dna matches and records unresolved off-scale vocabulary.
questions: Should declarations that mix var(--dna-) with raw px tokens always count as fully addressed?
Should shorthand exact scale values be tracked separately from genuinely off-scale values?
Should the scanner remain regex-based for CSS embedded in JavaScript, or use a parser-aware path per file type?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it enforces standalone geometry literals with exact --dna address equivalents and reports off-scale geometry as a ledger.
intention_signals: The script appears intended to keep chrome geometry tied to derived DNA tokens while preserving visibility into spacing, type, and radius values that have not yet been folded into the scale vocabulary.
novelty: It combines a failing exact-match linter with a non-failing off-scale ledger in the same scan pass.
contribution: Provides a file-local measurement and enforcement pass for geometry token adoption, including generated-scale extraction, surface scanning, exemptions, generated-block skipping, fixable violation reporting, and off-scale vocabulary accounting.
summary_for_embedding: Node ESM lint script that scans configured chrome surfaces for raw geometry px declarations, maps exact matches to --dna addresses, fails fixable standalone literals, and reports off-scale spacing/type/radius values.
intention_for_embedding: Created to make geometry values function like design-token addresses while keeping a live ledger of unresolved off-scale vocabulary for later rollout.

symbols.does: Extracts px-valued --dna variables from vars.css into a number-to-address map.
symbols.description: The function receives a regex with capture groups for variable name and px value, iterates over all matches in vars.css, and returns an object keyed by numeric px value. Each value is the corresponding CSS custom property address prefixed with --.

symbols.does: Returns the geometry class name for a declaration property or null when it is not policed.
symbols.description: The function walks the CLASS configuration and tests the property name against each class regex. It is the central gate that decides whether a declaration belongs to spacing, radius, or type before px scanning continues.

symbols.does: Scans one configured surface file for policed px declarations and records fixable, addressed, exempt, and off-scale findings.
symbols.description: The function resolves the surface path, skips missing files, reads source text, builds generated-block ranges, and applies the declaration regex. For each policed declaration, it handles env and px exemptions, detects existing --dna usage, records standalone exact-address literals as fixable, and updates the off-scale ledger and per-file reports.

symbols.does: Checks whether a source index falls inside any configured generated block range.
symbols.description: This local helper closes over genRanges inside scan and returns true when the declaration start index is within a marked generated region. It prevents generated DNA vars, fallbacks, and icons blocks from being linted as authored geometry.
